visualGoal: Make the reader measure their room before shopping, and show — with real product proof — how ceiling/depth/width each pick a different launch monitor and projector. Shallow-room camera vs deep-room radar vs tall-but-shallow overhead, all anchored on verified photos.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The overhead unit the 'tall-but-shallow' section is built around. Currently text-only (blockquote + Amazon search) because no verified image. Source a real photo before it can become a gearpick/shotgrid figure.
Second overhead reference (9'6" ceiling spec). Text-only until a real photo is sourced.

heroImage.notes: Existing hero at public/images/golf/golf-simulator-room-size.jpg — golfer at top of backswing near a low ceiling. On-message for the ceiling-strike thesis. Reused, not regenerated.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic home garage/basement being tape-measured for a golf simulator: a person holding a tape measure to the ceiling, painter's tape marking a projector mount point and hitting position on the floor, an open wall left clear for a real launch-monitor product overlay on a mat, warm practical lighting, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake launch monitor, warped room geometry, impossible ceiling, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: Room dimension to launch-monitor type
content: 9 ft ceiling shallow room = side camera (SkyTrak+)
10 ft ceiling deep 18-20 ft = radar or anything (R10)
10 ft ceiling tall but short = overhead (EYE XO2) or side-of-tee (R50)
Tight everywhere = side camera + accept compromise
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, golf greens palette, real product photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Throw distance = throw ratio x screen width
content: 0.5 TR: 12 ft screen from under 6 ft (shallow room)
Safe mount window 8-14 ft from screen
Under 8 ft ball hits lens / past 14 ft you shadow the screen
infographicPrompts.style: Side-view room diagram with beam path, numbered mount window, projector-photo placeholder for Codex overlay

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult golf curator Nina Brooks in a partly-built home simulator bay, tape measure in hand held to the ceiling near a garage-door track, confident measure-twice energy, product overlay space beside her on the mat, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

openQuestions: Do we want to source real photos for Uneekor EYE XO2 and Foresight Falcon so the overhead section carries visuals instead of text-only blockquotes?
Is there an existing verified TrackMan iO image we could add as a second overhead reference, or is it also text-only?
